introduction:
In Maharashtra, applying for a Driving Licence is a two-step process: obtaining a Learner's Licence (LL) and then applying for a Permanent Driving Licence (DL).

The Maharashtra Motor Vehicles Department offers Faceless Services for the Learner's Licence via Aadhaar e-KYC. This means you do not need to visit the RTO for your LL test.
quickFacts:
Crucial Path
  • Stage 1 (LL): Valid for 6 months. Apply at age 16 for non-gear, 18 for LMV.
  • Stage 2 (DL): Mandatory waiting period of 30 days after getting LL before applying for DL.
  • Test Track: Physical skill test at RTO is mandatory for permanent DL.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

For Learner's Licence (LL)
  • Aadhaar Card (For Faceless e-KYC)
  • Age Proof (Birth Certificate / SSC Certificate)
  • Address Proof (Voter ID / Utility Bill)
  • Form 1 (Self-declaration for physical fitness)
For Permanent DL
  • Original Learner's Licence
  • Application Form 4
  • Fee Payment Receipt
  • Medical Certificate Form 1-A (if applying for Transport Vehicle or age > 40)
onlineApplication:
How to Apply Online

Step 1: Get Your Learner's Licence (LL)
  1. Visit sarathi.parivahan.gov.in, the official Ministry of Road Transport portal.
  2. Select Maharashtra from the state list.
  3. Click on "Apply for Learner Licence".
  4. Select "Applicant holds neither DL nor LL" and proceed with Authentication via Aadhaar for the faceless process.
  5. Fill the application details, upload required documents and digital signature.
  6. Pay the required fee online (approx ₹150 - ₹200).
  7. Watch the mandatory Road Safety Tutorial Video on the portal.
  8. Take the LL computer test online from home. If passed, download your Learner's Licence instantly.
Step 2: Get Your Permanent Driving Licence (DL)
  1. Exactly 30 days after your LL issue date, return to the Sarathi portal.
  2. Click on "Apply for Driving Licence".
  3. Enter your Learner's Licence Number and Date of Birth.
  4. Fill the application form and pay the DL Smart Card & Test fee via GRAS (approx ₹700 - ₹900).
  5. Book your Driving Skill Test slot at your chosen Maharashtra RTO.
offlineApplication:
How to Apply Offline / Driving Test

The Learner's test is online, but the permanent DL test must be done at the RTO:

  1. Visit your designated Maharashtra RTO strictly 15-30 minutes before your booked time slot.
  2. Bring your original documents: Learner's Licence, ID proofs, payment receipt, and vehicle documents (RC, Insurance, PUC).
  3. Display the "L" board (red on white background) on your vehicle.
  4. Appear before the Motor Vehicle Inspector (MVI) on the track. In major cities, tests are evaluated by automated sensors.
afterApplying: Once Approved: The MVI will digitally approve your test. The physical Smart Card Driving Licence will be dispatched to your registered home address via Speed Post within 15-20 days.
helpfulLinks: MoRTH Sarathi Portal Maharashtra Transport Dept
